frame_analyses.description: Initial view of FLS trainer showing clean workspace with no instruments visible
Needle holder and grasper visible, suture being loaded onto needle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument positioning for needle loading

frame_analyses.description: Continued needle loading with both instruments engaged
Needle being passed through first mark on Penrose drain
Needle passing through second mark on Penrose drain
Beginning to form first throw of surgeon's knot
Continuing formation of surgeon's knot first throw
Tightening surgeon's knot with appropriate tension
Beginning second throw with hand switch
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching from first to second throw

frame_analyses.description: Forming single throw for second throw
Task appears complete with knot secured on Penrose drain
Final view showing completed knot on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture ends still visible, indicating cutting not yet performed

knot_assessments.notes: Appropriate hand switch and single throw

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain properly closed with no visible gaps

technique_summary: Task appears to have been completed with proper technique but exceeded the maximum time limit of 600 seconds. Only two throws were observed instead of the required three throws.
improvement_suggestions: Work more efficiently to complete within time limit
Practice three-throw sequence to meet FLS requirements
Focus on faster knot tying technique
